input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete step-by-step plan for .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows.

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK (latest with MAUI support)

```powershell
# Install winget if needed (ships with Windows 11)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
```

Verify:
```powershell
dotnet --list-sdks
```

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# Discover available workloads dynamically
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes android, ios, maccatalyst sub-workloads)
dotnet workload install maui
```

Verify:
```powershell
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio 2022 (recommended) or Build Tools

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Or, if you only need build tools (no IDE):
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
```

Then add required VS components via the installer or CLI:

```powershell
# Modify the installation to add MAUI + Android workloads
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.WindowsAppSDK.Cs `
  --passive --norestart
```

## 4. Install & Configure Android SDK

The VS installer typically handles this automatically. If you need manual control:

```powershell
# Default location after VS install
$AndroidSdkPath = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"

# Set environment variable
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $AndroidSdkPath, "User")

# Discover available platforms & build-tools dynamically
& "$AndroidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Install required components (pick latest stable versions from list)
& "$AndroidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "platforms;android-35" `
  "build-tools;35.0.0" `
  "platform-tools" `
  "emulator" `
  "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Accept all licenses
& "$AndroidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
```

## 5. Create an Android Emulator

```powershell
# List available device definitions
& "$AndroidSdkPath\emulator\emulator.bat" -list-avds
& "$AndroidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" list device

# Create an AVD
& "$AndroidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "Pixel_API_35" `
  -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" `
  -d "pixel_7"
```

## 6. Enable Windows App SDK Target (for WinUI)

```powershell
# Ensure Developer Mode is on (required for sideloading)
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /t REG_DWORD /f /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /d 1

# Windows App SDK comes with the MAUI workload — verify:
dotnet workload list | Select-String "maui-windows"
```

## 7. Create & Run a MAUI Project

```powershell
# Discover MAUI templates
dotnet new list maui

# Create new app
dotnet new maui -n MyMauiApp
cd MyMauiApp

# Run on Windows (WinUI)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator
dotnet build -f net9.0-android
dotnet build -t:Run -f net9.0-android
```

## 8. Verify Everything

```powershell
# One-shot health check
dotnet workload list
dotnet --info
& "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list_installed
```

---

**Key notes:**
- Replace `net9.0` with your SDK version if different (`dotnet --version` to check).
- Android API level (`android-35`) should match what `sdkmanager --list` shows as latest stable.
- Hyper-V or WHPX must be enabled for fast Android emulation (`Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All`).
